title: Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex
tagline: Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex, Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Krishna Janmasthan Temple is a Hindu temple situated in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Krishna Janmasthan Temple is a Hindu temple situated in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. There are three main temples inside the premises -- Keshavdev temple which is dedicated to Krishna, Garbh Griha where Krishna is believed to be born in Dvapar Yuga and Bhagvata Bhavan where presiding deities are Radha Krishna. The place has held religious significance since at least the 6th century BCE with findings of religious artifacts in excavations. The temples were destroyed multiple times throughout history, most recently by the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb in 1670.

topPlaces.name: Jama Mosque, Mathura
topPlaces.description: The Jama Mosque (Masjid) is a historical 17th-century congregational mosque in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by Abd-al-Nabi Khan, governor of Mathura during the reign of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb.

topPlaces.name: Shahi Eidgah Mosque
topPlaces.description: Shahi Eidgah Mosque is a 17th-century mosque in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India, built under the orders of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in 1670. It stands adjacent to the Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex, a site revered as the birthplace of the Hindu deity Krishna.

topPlaces.name: Mathura
topPlaces.description: Mathura (Braj pronunciation: [mɐ.t̪ʰʊ.ɾäː] ) is a city and the administrative headquarters of Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 162 kilometres (101 mi) south-east of Delhi; and about 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from the town of Vrindavan.

topPlaces.name: Dwarkadhish Temple, Mathura
topPlaces.description: Shri Dwarkadhish Temple is a Hindu temple in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. In this historic temple, Krishna is worshipped in his Dwarkanath or Dwarkadhish form along with his feminine counterpart goddess Radha in the form of Radharani.

topPlaces.name: Kankali Tila
topPlaces.description: Kankali Tila (also Kankali mound or Jaini mound) is a mound located at Mathura in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The name of the mound is derived from a modern temple of Hindu goddess Kankali.

topPlaces.name: Kankali Tila tablet of Sodasa
topPlaces.description: The Kankali Tila tablet of Sodasa, also called the Iryavati stone tablet, or Amohini ayagapata, is a large stone slab discovered in Kankali (area of Mathura) which mentions the rule of the Northern Satraps ruler Sodasa in Mathura. The tablet in the State Museum Lucknow (room J.1).

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Mathura
topPlaces.description: Government Museum, Mathura, commonly referred to as Mathura museum, is an archaeological museum in Mathura city of Uttar Pradesh state in India. The museum was founded by then collector of the Mathura district, Sir F. S. Growse in 1874.

topPlaces.name: Vishram Ghat
topPlaces.description: Vishram Ghat is a ghat, a bath and worship place, on the banks of river Yamuna in Mathura, India. The traditional parikrama starts and ends at Vishram Ghat.

items.note: The Jama Mosque (Masjid) is a historical 17th-century congregational mosque in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by Abd-al-Nabi Khan, governor of Mathura during the reign of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb.

Shahi Eidgah Mosque is a 17th-century mosque in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India, built under the orders of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in 1670. It stands adjacent to the Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex, a site revered as the birthplace of the Hindu deity Krishna.

Mathura (Braj pronunciation: [mɐ.t̪ʰʊ.ɾäː] ) is a city and the administrative headquarters of Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located 162 kilometres (101 mi) south-east of Delhi; and about 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from the town of Vrindavan.

Shri Dwarkadhish Temple is a Hindu temple in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India. In this historic temple, Krishna is worshipped in his Dwarkanath or Dwarkadhish form along with his feminine counterpart goddess Radha in the form of Radharani.
Kankali Tila (also Kankali mound or Jaini mound) is a mound located at Mathura in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The name of the mound is derived from a modern temple of Hindu goddess Kankali.

The Kankali Tila tablet of Sodasa, also called the Iryavati stone tablet, or Amohini ayagapata, is a large stone slab discovered in Kankali (area of Mathura) which mentions the rule of the Northern Satraps ruler Sodasa in Mathura. The tablet in the State Museum Lucknow (room J.1).

Government Museum, Mathura, commonly referred to as Mathura museum, is an archaeological museum in Mathura city of Uttar Pradesh state in India. The museum was founded by then collector of the Mathura district, Sir F. S. Growse in 1874.
Vishram Ghat is a ghat, a bath and worship place, on the banks of river Yamuna in Mathura, India. The traditional parikrama starts and ends at Vishram Ghat.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex?
faq.a: The best time to visit Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (50 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex?
faq.a: Stays in Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Krishna Janmasthan Temple Complex?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jama Mosque, Mathura, Shahi Eidgah Mosque, Mathura, Dwarkadhish Temple, Mathura, Kankali Tila.
